Investigating new-rig routing/identity path around AddRig route registration, ResolveRoutingTarget/ResolveHookDir, and rig metadata consistency for town-root dispatch.\n\nReproduction from mayor session on 2026-04-13: after force-unslinging gt-mh2 from gastown/rust, detailed status reported rust idle with no issue while recovery checks still reported NEEDS_RECOVERY (has_unpushed) and witness classified it as preserved recovery state. 'gt sling gt-mh2 gastown --dry-run' reported that a fresh polecat would be spawned, but the real 'gt sling gt-mh2 gastown' reused rust anyway, burned stale molecule gt-wisp-c4q7, created gt-wisp-617v, and reattached gt-mh2 to rust on a new branch. This confirms dry-run/live target-selection divergence and unsafe reuse of a recovery-only polecat."}

Repair the new-rig dispatch path from rig creation through witness cleanup so a newly added rig can create a bead, resolve it from town root, sling it to a polecat, manage the polecat consistently, and complete cleanup without manual database, routing, or lifecycle repair. Scope includes town-root routing, formula context selection, spawn atomicity, polecat registry consistency, and witness shutdown-task resolution after partial failures.","notes":"Findings: witness lifecycle shutdown currently only acknowledges reassignment and does not create or consult cleanup wisps despite sling promising cleanup/defer behavior. Investigating new-rig routing/identity path around AddRig route registration, ResolveRoutingTarget/ResolveHookDir, and rig metadata consistency for town-root dispatch.

Reproduction from mayor session on 2026-04-13: after force-unslinging gt-mh2 from gastown/rust, detailed status reported rust idle with no issue while recovery checks still reported NEEDS_RECOVERY (has_unpushed) and witness classified it as preserved recovery state. 'gt sling gt-mh2 gastown --dry-run' reported that a fresh polecat would be spawned, but the real 'gt sling gt-mh2 gastown' reused rust anyway, burned stale molecule gt-wisp-c4q7, created gt-wisp-617v, and reattached gt-mh2 to rust on a new branch.

Additional runtime failure: captured gt-rust session output shows the worker printed 'I'm sorry, but I cannot assist with that request.' instead of continuing work on the hooked bead, leaving gt-mh2 active but unadvanced. This is now part of the mayor-side reproduction context for dispatch/recovery robustness.","status":"hooked","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/rust","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-13T12:37:51Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-13T12:58:17Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Mayor launcher analysis: the fresh-polecat failure is not explained by the embedded gastown wrapper script. The installed runtime wrapper actually used at /home/coder/.local/bin/gt-opencode comes from dotfiles and diverges from gastown's embedded internal/wrappers/scripts/gt-opencode. The installed wrapper runs a custom gt_prime_town path that executes 'gt prime' with town-root/Dolt env rewriting, not the upstream-style 'gt prime --hook' handoff. Manual probing shows that wrapper launches OpenCode and immediately re-enters Gastown mayor context, which matches the fresh-nitro startup misbehavior. This strongly implicates dotfiles-installed wrapper drift as a root cause for worker-launch miswiring."}

new_value: {"notes":"Found the contamination path in internal/polecat/session_manager.go: session startup called DefaultBranch() on a normal worktree, which resolves the current local branch. new_value: {"design":"Fix keeps fresh branch creation pinned to canonical remote refs. Session startup now resolves a canonical start point as origin/\u003cconfigured default branch\u003e (falling back to the remote default branch), fetches origin, and only mints a fresh branch from that ref. It preserves the current branch when respawning the same issue branch, but if a recovered worktree is sitting on main/master or an unrelated preserved polecat/* branch for a new issue, it creates a new issue branch from the canonical base instead of inheriting local history. Regression tests cover AddWithOptions (fresh spawn), ReuseIdlePolecat (idle reuse), and session recovery branch bootstrapping."}

When two  commands target the same rig concurrently, the dispatcher can reuse the same idle polecat for both requests and then time out acquiring the assignee sling lock instead of allocating a second worker or failing cleanly before formula side effects occur. Reproduced during the first bootstrap remediation wave on beads/quartz, gastown/fury, and coder_dotfiles/furiosa. Fix dispatch selection/locking so same-rig concurrent slings either serialize before polecat assignment or reserve distinct polecats atomically, and ensure stale formula wisps are not left behind on the failed attempt."}

{"design":"Fork-only OpenCode refusal contract:
- Scope: all OpenCode sessions that load the Gastown .opencode plugin.
- Trigger: exact assistant completion text "Im sorry, but I cannot assist with that request." only.
- Retry payload: exact user message "Continue" only.
- Timing: retry immediately with no cooldown, delay, or backoff.
- State: consecutive refusal counter is session-scoped.
- Reset: any non-refusal assistant completion resets the counter to zero.
- Escalation: the fifth consecutive refusal escalates instead of sending a sixth retry."}

Write the exact behavioral contract for the fork-only OpenCode refusal plugin. Specify the exact refusal string to match, that scope is all OpenCode sessions, that the retry message is exactly Continue, that retries happen immediately with no cooldown or backoff, that the counter is session-scoped, that any non-refusal assistant completion resets the counter, and that the fifth consecutive refusal escalates instead of triggering a sixth retry.","design":"Fork-only OpenCode refusal contract:
- Scope: all OpenCode sessions that load the Gastown .opencode plugin.
- Trigger: exact assistant completion text "Im sorry, but I cannot assist with that request." only.
- Retry payload: exact user message "Continue" only.
- Timing: retry immediately with no cooldown, delay, or backoff.
- State: consecutive refusal counter is session-scoped.
- Reset: any non-refusal assistant completion resets the counter to zero.
- Escalation: the fifth consecutive refusal escalates instead of sending a sixth retry.","status":"hooked","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/polecats/fury","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-14T14:52:07Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:58:23Z"}

Reflogs show new polecat-local branches are being created from older polecat-local branches inside preserved worktrees instead of from a clean repo base. Examples: polecat/shiny/gt-85v@mnxfqn09 was created from polecat/shiny-mnxfoepj at 2026-04-13 16:57:04Z; polecat/rust-mnxfkbm4 was created from polecat/rust-mnxc3qtn at 2026-04-13 16:52:10Z. This explains why the unrelated commit b9428194 keeps reappearing even on supposedly fresh paths: spawn/restart is deriving from stale local polecat refs rather than a canonical base branch. {"id":"gt-nfe","title":"Fix concurrent same-rig sling idle-polecat contention","description":"attached_molecule: gt-wisp-3ee3
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-14T14:11:12Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=upstream-rebuild-main","base_branch=upstream-rebuild-main"]

formula_vars: base_branch=upstream-rebuild-main
base_branch=upstream-rebuild-main

When two  commands target the same rig concurrently, the dispatcher can reuse the same idle polecat for both requests and then time out acquiring the assignee sling lock instead of allocating a second worker or failing cleanly before formula side effects occur. Reproduced during the first bootstrap remediation wave on beads/quartz, gastown/fury, and coder_dotfiles/furiosa. Fix dispatch selection/locking so same-rig concurrent slings either serialize before polecat assignment or reserve distinct polecats atomically, and ensure stale formula wisps are not left behind on the failed attempt.","notes":"Findings: serialized idle-polecat reuse per rig in SpawnPolecatForSling so concurrent same-rig slings cannot grab the same idle worker; added rollback on assignee-lock failure so pre-hook formula wisps and spawned polecats are cleaned up instead of leaving stale attached_molecule state. Verification: focused internal/cmd tests passed with GOTOOLCHAIN=auto; full internal/cmd suite is blocked here by unrelated patrol Docker/rootless test setup.","status":"hooked","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/nitro","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-14T14:08:59Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:09:13Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Follow-up findings: explicit agent overrides were still unstable after the opencode prompt-mode fix. ResolveAgentConfigWithOverride could be shadowed by same-named custom agents, and subcommand overrides like 'opencode acp' leaked the full override string into GT_AGENT. Fixed override parsing so built-in names stay pinned to their built-in runtime, set ResolvedAgent on override resolution, and ensured startup commands export the base agent name for GT_AGENT/GT_PROCESS_NAMES. Verification: go test ./internal/config ./internal/runtime; go test ./internal/mail -run 'TestDetectTownRoot|TestDetectTownRoot_PrefersEnvVar|TestResolveBeadsDir|TestSendFromCrewWorkspace_AvoidsEphemeralPrefixMismatch|TestNewRouterWithTownRoot|TestValidateRecipientFilesystemFallback|TestValidateRecipientFilesystemFallbackWithRouteErrors'"}

new_value: {"notes":"Updated findings: startup hook failures on OpenCode polecats are reproducible from stale installed .opencode/plugins/gastown.js files that still run plain 'gt prime' instead of 'gt prime --hook'. Manual 'gt prime --hook' succeeds from the polecat repo root, so the session is recoverable. Fixed the OpenCode template to use hook-aware priming for autonomous roles, updated the compaction hint, and taught InstallForRole to auto-upgrade stale OpenCode plugins on next startup so existing polecat worktrees recover without manual hook sync."}

new_value: {"notes":"Root cause confirmed in OpenCode startup path: internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js primed fresh and compacted sessions with plain gt prime instead of gt prime --hook, unlike the shared hook config. Fixed the template to always build gt prime --hook with GT_HOOK_SOURCE/GT_SESSION_ID metadata, aligned the compaction reminder text, and added coverage for session.created, session.compacted, and transform fallback paths in TestOpenCodePluginRefusalAutoContinue. Verification: go test ./internal/hooks and go test ./internal/hooks -run TestOpenCodePluginRefusalAutoContinue pass. Full rebased go test ./... is not pre-verifiable in this environment because unchanged cmd/gt cross-platform build tests fail in internal/util/diskspace_unix.go and multiple packages require rootless Docker/testcontainers."}

Multiple newly slung polecat sessions across gastown and beads are checking inbox or opening context, then immediately responding with a generic refusal instead of executing the hooked bead. This reproduces the known startup refusal class in live work and is now blocking most dispatched implementation. {"id":"gt-fuc.6","title":"Fix polecat startup refusal loop in hooked gastown work sessions","description":"base_branch=upstream-rebuild-main

Multiple newly slung polecat sessions across gastown and beads are checking inbox or opening context, then immediately responding with a generic refusal instead of executing the hooked bead. This reproduces the known startup refusal class in live work and is now blocking most dispatched implementation. Investigate startup prompt assembly, inherited conversation state, runtime/provider selection, and hook priming so a fresh working session starts acting on its hook instead of refusing.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Freshly slung sessions execute hooked work instead of emitting generic refusal replies
- [ ] The fix covers live reproduction on currently affected gastown and beads polecats
- [ ] Startup prompt assembly and runtime selection have one clear source of truth for hooked execution behavior","notes":"Root cause confirmed in OpenCode startup path: internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js primed fresh and compacted sessions with plain gt prime instead of gt prime --hook, unlike the shared hook config. Fixed the template to always build gt prime --hook with GT_HOOK_SOURCE/GT_SESSION_ID metadata, aligned the compaction reminder text, and added coverage for session.created, session.compacted, and transform fallback paths in TestOpenCodePluginRefusalAutoContinue. Verification: go test ./internal/hooks and go test ./internal/hooks -run TestOpenCodePluginRefusalAutoContinue pass. Full rebased go test ./... is not pre-verifiable in this environment because unchanged cmd/gt cross-platform build tests fail in internal/util/diskspace_unix.go and multiple packages require rootless Docker/testcontainers.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-15T14:28:19Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-16T01:38:05Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Found a startup priming split-brain in the OpenCode path: generated SessionStart hooks already run 'gt prime --hook', but the OpenCode plugin template still injects bare 'gt prime' into system context. This means hooked sessions can miss the hooked-work/autonomous startup path even when the startup beacon tells them to use --hook. Working on unifying the plugin to use the same priming command and adding regression coverage around the emitted command."}

Multiple newly slung polecat sessions across gastown and beads are checking inbox or opening context, then immediately responding with a generic refusal instead of executing the hooked bead. This reproduces the known startup refusal class in live work and is now blocking most dispatched implementation. Investigate startup prompt assembly, inherited conversation state, runtime/provider selection, and hook priming so a fresh working session starts acting on its hook instead of refusing.","design":"Found the target branch still used bare 'gt prime' in internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js, so hooked OpenCode sessions could miss hook-mode startup behavior despite hook config already using 'gt prime --hook'. Updated the plugin template to call 'gt prime --hook' and added regression coverage in internal/hooks/opencode_plugin_test.go to verify polecat startup still injects 'gt mail check --inject' while crew startup only primes with '--hook'. Verified with: go test ./internal/hooks/...","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Freshly slung sessions execute hooked work instead of emitting generic refusal replies
- [ ] The fix covers live reproduction on currently affected gastown and beads polecats
- [ ] Startup prompt assembly and runtime selection have one clear source of truth for hooked execution behavior","notes":"Verified root cause: the OpenCode startup plugin still invoked bare 'gt prime' during initial context injection, which diverged from the hooked SessionStart path and could skip hook-mode startup behavior. Switched the plugin template to 'gt prime --hook' and added regression coverage in internal/hooks/opencode_plugin_test.go to verify polecat and crew startup priming, while only autonomous roles still inject 'gt mail check --inject'. Verification: go test ./internal/hooks/... passes.","status":"hooked","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/nitro","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-15T14:28:19Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-16T03:00:35Z"}

This reproduces the known startup refusal class in live work and is now blocking most dispatched implementation. Investigate startup prompt assembly, inherited conversation state, runtime/provider selection, and hook priming so a fresh working session starts acting on its hook instead of refusing.","design":"Found the target branch still used bare 'gt prime' in internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js, so hooked OpenCode sessions could miss hook-mode startup behavior despite hook config already using 'gt prime --hook'. Updated the plugin template to call 'gt prime --hook' and added regression coverage in internal/hooks/opencode_plugin_test.go to verify polecat startup still injects 'gt mail check --inject' while crew startup only primes with '--hook'. Verified with: go test ./internal/hooks/...","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Freshly slung sessions execute hooked work instead of emitting generic refusal replies
- [ ] The fix covers live reproduction on currently affected gastown and beads polecats
- [ ] Startup prompt assembly and runtime selection have one clear source of truth for hooked execution behavior","notes":"Confirmed root cause in this worktree: internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js still loaded startup context with bare 'gt prime' even though hook SessionStart config already uses 'gt prime --hook'. Updated the plugin template to use '--hook' and extended the OpenCode plugin regression harness to assert polecat startup runs 'gt prime --hook' plus 'gt mail check --inject', while crew startup only runs 'gt prime --hook'.","status":"hooked","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/chrome","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-15T14:28:19Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-16T03:26:10Z"}

Multiple newly slung polecat sessions across gastown and beads are checking inbox or opening context, then immediately responding with a generic refusal instead of executing the hooked bead. This reproduces the known startup refusal class in live work and is now blocking most dispatched implementation. Investigate startup prompt assembly, inherited conversation state, runtime/provider selection, and hook priming so a fresh working session starts acting on its hook instead of refusing.","design":"Implemented the fix in the OpenCode startup plugin by switching internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js from 'gt prime' to 'gt prime --hook', matching the existing hook SessionStart config. Added regression assertions to internal/hooks/opencode_plugin_test.go so session.created now verifies polecat startup runs 'gt prime --hook' plus 'gt mail check --inject', while crew startup runs only 'gt prime --hook'. Verification: 'go test ./internal/hooks/...' passes before and after rebase. Full 'go test ./...' does not pass in this environment due to unrelated failures outside this diff: cmd/gt TestCrossPlatformBuild (freebsd type mismatch in internal/util/diskspace_unix.go), internal/config TestResolveAgentConfigWithOverride/override_with_subcommand, and multiple packages that require rootless Docker/testcontainers.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Freshly slung sessions execute hooked work instead of emitting generic refusal replies
- [ ] The fix covers live reproduction on currently affected gastown and beads polecats
- [ ] Startup prompt assembly and runtime selection have one clear source of truth for hooked execution behavior","notes":"Confirmed root cause in this worktree: internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js still loaded startup context with bare 'gt prime' even though hook SessionStart config already uses 'gt prime --hook'. Updated the plugin template to use '--hook' and extended the OpenCode plugin regression harness to assert polecat startup runs 'gt prime --hook' plus 'gt mail check --inject', while crew startup only runs 'gt prime --hook'.","status":"hooked","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/chrome","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-15T14:28:19Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-16T03:31:40Z"}

Investigate startup prompt assembly, inherited conversation state, runtime/provider selection, and hook priming so a fresh working session starts acting on its hook instead of refusing.","design":"Implemented the fix in the OpenCode startup plugin by switching internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js from 'gt prime' to 'gt prime --hook', matching the existing hook SessionStart config. Added regression assertions to internal/hooks/opencode_plugin_test.go so session.created now verifies polecat startup runs 'gt prime --hook' plus 'gt mail check --inject', while crew startup runs only 'gt prime --hook'. Verification: 'go test ./internal/hooks/...' passes before and after rebase. Full 'go test ./...' does not pass in this environment due to unrelated failures outside this diff: cmd/gt TestCrossPlatformBuild (freebsd type mismatch in internal/util/diskspace_unix.go), internal/config TestResolveAgentConfigWithOverride/override_with_subcommand, and multiple packages that require rootless Docker/testcontainers.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Freshly slung sessions execute hooked work instead of emitting generic refusal replies
- [ ] The fix covers live reproduction on currently affected gastown and beads polecats
- [ ] Startup prompt assembly and runtime selection have one clear source of truth for hooked execution behavior","notes":"Confirmed the live bug still existed in this worktree: internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js was loading startup context with bare 'gt prime', while internal/hooks/config.go already uses 'gt prime --hook' for SessionStart. Fixed the OpenCode template to call 'gt prime --hook' and extended internal/hooks/opencode_plugin_test.go to assert session.created startup runs '/bin/sh -lc gt prime --hook' plus '/bin/sh -lc gt mail check --inject' for polecats, and only hooked priming for crew sessions. Verification: go test ./internal/hooks/...","status":"hooked","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/nitro","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-15T14:28:19Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-16T13:20:49Z"}

Multiple newly slung polecat sessions across gastown and beads are checking inbox or opening context, then immediately responding with a generic refusal instead of executing the hooked bead. This reproduces the known startup refusal class in live work and is now blocking most dispatched implementation. Investigate startup prompt assembly, inherited conversation state, runtime/provider selection, and hook priming so a fresh working session starts acting on its hook instead of refusing.","design":"Implemented the fix in the OpenCode startup plugin by switching internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js from 'gt prime' to 'gt prime --hook', matching the existing hook SessionStart config. Added regression assertions to internal/hooks/opencode_plugin_test.go so session.created now verifies polecat startup runs 'gt prime --hook' plus 'gt mail check --inject', while crew startup runs only 'gt prime --hook'. Verification: 'go test ./internal/hooks/...' passes before and after rebase. Full 'go test ./...' does not pass in this environment due to unrelated failures outside this diff: cmd/gt TestCrossPlatformBuild (freebsd type mismatch in internal/util/diskspace_unix.go), internal/config TestResolveAgentConfigWithOverride/override_with_subcommand, and multiple packages that require rootless Docker/testcontainers.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Freshly slung sessions execute hooked work instead of emitting generic refusal replies
- [ ] The fix covers live reproduction on currently affected gastown and beads polecats
- [ ] Startup prompt assembly and runtime selection have one clear source of truth for hooked execution behavior","notes":"Recovery attempt after gt done preserved the sandbox: branch polecat/nitro-mo1i8pl7 is clean and still contains commit 2ff40907. Push remains blocked by missing GitHub credentials in this session. 'gh auth status' reports no logged-in hosts, and direct push attempts to both origin (Bella-Giraffety/gastown) and upstream (gastownhall/gastown) fail with 'Invalid username or token'.","status":"hooked","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/nitro","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-15T14:28:19Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-16T13:38:02Z"}

Multiple newly slung polecat sessions across gastown and beads are checking inbox or opening context, then immediately responding with a generic refusal instead of executing the hooked bead. This reproduces the known startup refusal class in live work and is now blocking most dispatched implementation. Investigate startup prompt assembly, inherited conversation state, runtime/provider selection, and hook priming so a fresh working session starts acting on its hook instead of refusing.","design":"Implemented the fix in the OpenCode startup plugin by switching internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js from 'gt prime' to 'gt prime --hook', matching the existing hook SessionStart config. Added regression assertions to internal/hooks/opencode_plugin_test.go so session.created now verifies polecat startup runs 'gt prime --hook' plus 'gt mail check --inject', while crew startup runs only 'gt prime --hook'. Verification: 'go test ./internal/hooks/...' passes before and after rebase. Full 'go test ./...' does not pass in this environment due to unrelated failures outside this diff: cmd/gt TestCrossPlatformBuild (freebsd type mismatch in internal/util/diskspace_unix.go), internal/config TestResolveAgentConfigWithOverride/override_with_subcommand, and multiple packages that require rootless Docker/testcontainers.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Freshly slung sessions execute hooked work instead of emitting generic refusal replies
- [ ] The fix covers live reproduction on currently affected gastown and beads polecats
- [ ] Startup prompt assembly and runtime selection have one clear source of truth for hooked execution behavior","notes":"Recovery attempt after gt done preserved the sandbox: branch polecat/nitro-mo1i8pl7 is clean and still contains commit 2ff40907. Investigate startup prompt assembly, inherited conversation state, runtime/provider selection, and hook priming so a fresh working session starts acting on its hook instead of refusing.","design":"Implemented the fix in the OpenCode startup plugin by switching internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js from 'gt prime' to 'gt prime --hook', matching the existing hook SessionStart config. Verification: 'go test ./internal/hooks/...' passes before and after rebase. Full 'go test ./...' does not pass in this environment due to unrelated failures outside this diff: cmd/gt TestCrossPlatformBuild (freebsd type mismatch in internal/util/diskspace_unix.go), internal/config TestResolveAgentConfigWithOverride/override_with_subcommand, and multiple packages that require rootless Docker/testcontainers.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Freshly slung sessions execute hooked work instead of emitting generic refusal replies
- [ ] The fix covers live reproduction on currently affected gastown and beads polecats
- [ ] Startup prompt assembly and runtime selection have one clear source of truth for hooked execution behavior","notes":"Verified on branch polecat/rust-mo1jqikb after rebasing onto origin/upstream-rebuild-main. The central OpenCode plugin exists in mayor and deacon and contains the current refusal auto-continue logic, but doctor output flagged missing gastown.js plugin files in multiple polecat workspaces. This makes the try-again / continue-nudge path impossible to trust end to end even if the central implementation is correct. The provisioning/validation path for polecat workspaces needs to be repaired and then verified against current hooked-work refusal behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Polecat workspaces that should carry the OpenCode plugin actually receive gastown.js
- [ ] The current plugin implementation with refusal auto-continue is the version provisioned into worker environments
- [ ] Refusal retry/continue behavior can be validated from a real polecat-style startup path instead of only from mayor/deacon copies","notes":"Root cause: hooks sync and doctor validation discover rig worktrees one level deep, which works for crew/<name> but misses polecat worktrees at polecats/<name>/<rig>. {"id":"gt-fuc.1","title":"Fix gastown sling, polecat persistence, and witness cleanup regressions","description":"base_branch=upstream-rebuild-main

Reconcile the fork with upstream and local hardening so sling/new-rig dispatch, hook assignment, polecat persistence, and witness cleanup behave consistently after partial failures. This task should cover the empty-hook, missing-worktree, and stale lifecycle state class already observed in this town.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Sling does not leave empty-hook or partially spawned polecat state after failure
- [ ] Polecat registry and preserved branch behavior stay consistent through startup and cleanup
- [ ] Witness shutdown and cleanup logic resolves stale lifecycle state instead of only acknowledging it","notes":"Updated findings: startup hook failures on OpenCode polecats are reproducible from stale installed .opencode/plugins/gastown.js files that still run plain 'gt prime' instead of 'gt prime --hook'. Multiple newly slung polecat sessions across gastown and beads are checking inbox or opening context, then immediately responding with a generic refusal instead of executing the hooked bead. This reproduces the known startup refusal class in live work and is now blocking most dispatched implementation. Investigate startup prompt assembly, inherited conversation state, runtime/provider selection, and hook priming so a fresh working session starts acting on its hook instead of refusing.","design":"Implemented the fix in the OpenCode startup plugin by switching internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js from 'gt prime' to 'gt prime --hook', matching the existing hook SessionStart config. Added regression assertions to internal/hooks/opencode_plugin_test.go so session.created now verifies polecat startup runs 'gt prime --hook' plus 'gt mail check --inject', while crew startup runs only 'gt prime --hook'. Verification: 'go test ./internal/hooks/...' passes before and after rebase. Full 'go test ./...' does not pass in this environment due to unrelated failures outside this diff: cmd/gt TestCrossPlatformBuild (freebsd type mismatch in internal/util/diskspace_unix.go), internal/config TestResolveAgentConfigWithOverride/override_with_subcommand, and multiple packages that require rootless Docker/testcontainers.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Freshly slung sessions execute hooked work instead of emitting generic refusal replies
- [ ] The fix covers live reproduction on currently affected gastown and beads polecats
- [ ] Startup prompt assembly and runtime selection have one clear source of truth for hooked execution behavior","notes":"Verified on branch polecat/nitro-mo1my5io with commit d81ae7d1 after rebasing onto origin/upstream-rebuild-main. Fixed the OpenCode startup template to use gt prime --hook for hooked sessions, matching the existing SessionStart hook behavior. Added regression coverage in internal/hooks/opencode_plugin_test.go that exercises session.created plus experimental.chat.system.transform and asserts startup commands for polecat and crew roles. Verification: go test ./internal/hooks/... passes. Submitting without --pre-verified because I did not rerun the repository's full configured gate suite in this session.","status":"hooked","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/nitro","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-15T14:28:19Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-16T15:34:06Z"}

Verification: 'go test ./internal/hooks/...' passes before and after rebase. This gap must be tracked separately so we do not over-claim completion from the hooks-sync fix alone.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] The authoritative provisioned gastown.js template is confirmed to include the intended refusal auto-continue behavior, or the acceptance/spec wording is corrected
- [ ] Provisioning and behavior expectations are reconciled so worker environments get the same plugin logic that acceptance depends on
- [ ] Any remaining distinction between provisioning correctness and behavioral validation is documented and linked","notes":"Five-way review summary: gt-tz5 successfully restores refusal auto-continue logic into the authoritative embedded/provisioned OpenCode template, but it is not the final end state for the refusal stream. Review found three residual gaps: (1) lastHandledMessageID is recorded before assistant text fetch succeeds, so a transient fetch failure can permanently suppress handling for that message; (2) the restored template still gates behavior to autonomous roles, which appears narrower than the historical all-OpenCode-session fix; and (3) current regression coverage proves template content, not runtime/event behavior, so contract confidence still depends on gt-jbf and additional follow-up fixes. Treat gt-tz5 as preserved submit-ready for the template restoration slice only, with the residual correctness/test gaps tracked separately.","status":"hooked","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/fury","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-16T16:48:35Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-16T21:53:51Z"}

{"id":"gt-tz5","title":"Align provisioned gastown.js template with refusal auto-continue expectations","description":"attached_molecule: gt-wisp-f0er
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-16T22:59:00Z

Review of gt-hii confirmed a spec/implementation gap: the nested polecat plugin provisioning fix is valid, but the authoritative embedded OpenCode template being provisioned does not obviously carry the refusal auto-continue / cooldown logic that the acceptance stream expects. That means provisioning can be correct while the actual Continue behavior remains unproven or absent in worker environments. This gap must be tracked separately so we do not over-claim completion from the hooks-sync fix alone.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] The authoritative provisioned gastown.js template is confirmed to include the intended refusal auto-continue behavior, or the acceptance/spec wording is corrected
- [ ] Provisioning and behavior expectations are reconciled so worker environments get the same plugin logic that acceptance depends on
- [ ] Any remaining distinction between provisioning correctness and behavioral validation is documented and linked","notes":"Five-way review summary: gt-tz5 successfully restores refusal auto-continue logic into the authoritative embedded/provisioned OpenCode template, but it is not the final end state for the refusal stream. Review found three residual gaps: (1) lastHandledMessageID is recorded before assistant text fetch succeeds, so a transient fetch failure can permanently suppress handling for that message; (2) the restored template still gates behavior to autonomous roles, which appears narrower than the historical all-OpenCode-session fix; and (3) current regression coverage proves template content, not runtime/event behavior, so contract confidence still depends on gt-jbf and additional follow-up fixes. Treat gt-tz5 as preserved submit-ready for the template restoration slice only, with the residual correctness/test gaps tracked separately.","status":"hooked","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/guzzle","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-16T16:48:35Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-16T22:59:04Z"}

old_value: {"id":"gt-1wc","title":"Make restored OpenCode refusal handler tolerate assistant-text fetch failure","description":"Review of gt-tz5 found a correctness bug in the restored OpenCode refusal handler: the plugin records lastHandledMessageID before fetchAssistantText succeeds. If the assistant-text lookup fails once for a completed refusal message, that message is permanently deduped and neither Continue retries nor escalation can ever happen for it. This should be fixed without broadening the scope of gt-tz5 itself.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] The refusal handler only records a message as handled after the assistant text is fetched successfully, or otherwise remains retryable on transient fetch failure\n- [ ] A regression test covers failed assistant-text fetch followed by successful retry for the same message\n- [ ] The change is limited to correctness of restored refusal handling, not broader plugin behavior changes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-16T21:53:50Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-16T21:53:50Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Root cause: hooks worktree discovery only returned immediate role children (for example polecats/fury), but polecat git worktrees live one level deeper at polecats/\u003cname\u003e/gastown. Fixed DiscoverWorktrees to prefer nested git worktree roots, and added regression tests for hooks discovery, gt hooks sync, and doctor hooks-sync checks covering nested polecat worktrees. Also corrected OpenCode plugin path docs to .opencode/plugins/gastown.js."}

new_value: {"notes":"Answer to the current classification question: this looks like a Gastown lifecycle/reuse bug, not a dotfiles setup bug. Evidence: the pattern appears at slot-reuse time inside already-created polecat clones; it produces zero-diff stale branches with no session and no local work, and it closely matches the existing upstream lifecycle family in gastownhall/gastown#3030. Dotfiles may influence startup noise globally, but they are not the most direct owner of this failed-start reuse loop."}
old_value: {"id":"gt-acu","title":"Handle repeated zero-work failed-start idle-reuse loops on reused polecats","description":"Repeated dispatches to certain reused polecat slots (most clearly beads/amber, but the same pattern has appeared on some gastown slots) produce a new branch name with zero local diff, no attached live issue, and no running session. This is not a dotfiles bootstrap symptom; it matches the broader idle-reuse / stale-session lifecycle family in Gastown, where reused polecat slots can fail to transition cleanly and leave behind misleading stale branches or no-op retries. Upstream issue gastownhall/gastown#3030 is closely related and likely shares root cause in reuse / session lifecycle handling.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Reusing an idle polecat slot either produces a real attached live session or cleanly fails without creating misleading zero-work stale branches\n- [ ] Failed-start reuse attempts are explicitly classified so the same bad slot does not keep absorbing new slings silently\n- [ ] Regression coverage or lifecycle checks cover repeated reuse of a bad slot without requiring witness-only cleanup","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"thalia.geraghty@fiserv.com","created_at":"2026-04-16T23:33:06Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-04-16T23:33:06Z"}
